content.full_text:
July 22, 1976


Robert L. Shand
City Clerk-Treasurer
P.O. Box 607
Downey, California  90241

Dear Mr. Shand:

     This will confirm our conversation in which I informed you of the requirements of the Political Reform Act as they relate to campaign disclosure filings of committees. A committee which raises contributions of $500 or more in a calendar year must file before and after the election. Such a committee would be required to disclose contributions and expenditures of $50 or more.

     Please feel free to call me if you have any further questions or comments about the Political Reform Act.

Very truly yours,


Robert M. Stern
General Counsel

RMS:ma

bcc:  J. Pritchard

City of Downey

CITY COUNCIL
RICHARD M. JENNINGS
MAYOR
JOSEPH E. DiLORETO
MAYOR PRO-TEM
WALTER S. TEMPLE
DONALD H. WINTON
HAZEL N. SCOTTO

CHARLES W. THOMPSON
CITY MANAGER
DONALD B. ROBISON
ASSISTANT CITY MANAGER
ROBERT L. SHAND
CITY CLERK - TREASURER
CITY HALL
861-0361

8425 SECOND STREET
DOWNEY, CALIFORNIA 90241
MAIL ADDRESS: P.O. BOX 607

June 30, 1976

Mr. Robert M. Stern
General Counsel
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Campaign Statements

Dear Mr. Stern:

Recently we corresponded with each other (your letter of May 25, 1976) regarding the CONCERNED CITIZENS COMMITTEE.

Prior to the filing deadline of June 17 for the third filing of disclosure of financial interest campaign committees in conjunction with the City of Downey general municipal election held on April 13, 1976, I received a telephone call from Mr. Edward DiLoreto, Treasurer of the Concerned Citizens Committee. Mr. DiLoreto stated he was going to send me a letter containing information regarding funds received by the Concerned Citizens Committee in conjunction with advertising in boosting various candidates in the April election.

Mr. DiLoreto reported the following receipts:

Concerned Citizens Committee          $223.10
Edward DiLoreto                       472.00
Yale Engineering Company             476.37

No campaign statement forms (420 or 430) were received from Mr. DiLoreto.

It is my understanding that Mr. DiLoreto is associated with the Yale Engineering Company in Downey. However, Mr. DiLoreto contends that no single amount from any given source donated to the Concerned Citizens Committee exceeded $500. For this reason, no campaign statement was filed on behalf of the Concerned Citizens Committee.

Mr. Robert M. Stern                2                June 30, 1976


I would appreciate an opinion from your office regarding the above
situation as to whether or not we should have on file a statement
from Mr. DiLoreto as Treasurer of the Concerned Citizens Committee.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
Robert L. Shand
City Clerk-Treasurer
